context: Tests whether the agent correctly integrates Avalara AvaTax including proper client initialization, SalesOrder vs SalesInvoice transaction types, correct line item tax codes, the two-phase estimate-then-commit workflow, transaction code storage, and order cancellation voiding.

checklist.name: Correct package
checklist.description: Uses the 'avatax' npm package (import/require from 'avatax'), not a generic HTTP client

checklist.name: Client initialization fields
checklist.description: Avalara client is initialized with all four fields: appName, appVersion, environment, and machineName

checklist.name: Sandbox vs production env
checklist.description: The 'environment' field is set to 'production' when AVALARA_ENV equals 'production', and 'sandbox' otherwise — not hardcoded to either

checklist.name: withSecurity() for credentials
checklist.description: Credentials (username and password) are passed via .withSecurity({ username, password }), not via constructor or other methods

checklist.name: SalesOrder for estimates
checklist.description: Tax estimate calls use transaction type 'SalesOrder' (commit=false), not 'SalesInvoice'

checklist.name: SalesInvoice for commits
checklist.description: Tax commit calls use transaction type 'SalesInvoice' (commit=true), not 'SalesOrder'

checklist.name: Shipping line FR010000
checklist.description: A separate shipping line item is included in Avalara transactions with taxCode 'FR010000'

checklist.name: Default tax code P0000000
checklist.description: Product lines without a specific tax code fall back to 'P0000000' (not null, empty, or a different code)

checklist.name: Transaction code persisted
checklist.description: The Avalara transaction code (result.code) is saved on the order record after calculation or commit

checklist.name: Commit on order confirmed
checklist.description: The commit step is triggered after order/payment confirmation (e.g., in an order.confirmed handler, post-payment webhook, or commit step), not during estimate/checkout

checklist.name: Void on cancellation
checklist.description: Order cancellation logic calls a void or cancel operation on the Avalara transaction (references commitTransaction or voidTransaction on the committed code)

checklist.name: Consistent estimate/commit params
checklist.description: The same address, line item, and shipping parameters are used for both the estimate and the final commit — no parameters differ between the two calls
